title: Parity-AUDIT 16 AST sanitizer banks + close 2 gaps surfaced by M1/M2
purpose: Per-bank parity audit of AST sanitizer banks against their corresponding regex sanitizer Vecs across 16 language banks. Resolve the 2 gaps surfaced by M1+M2 (cpp string-literal regression M2-FIND-01; TS Zod parse/safeParse missing) BEFORE M4 atomically deletes the regex banks.
implemented: AUDITED all 16 (regex Vec, AST bank) pairs in crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs. 14 langs at parity (Python, Go, Java, Rust, C, Ruby, Kotlin, Swift, CSharp, Scala, Php, Lua, Elixir, Ocaml). 2 gaps resolved (TypeScript+Cpp). Full per-lang verdict in reports/M3-audit.json.

FIXED Gap 2 (M1 finding): added 1 new AstSanitizerPattern to TYPESCRIPT_AST_SANITIZERS — `{ call_names: [], member_patterns: [("*","parse"),("*","safeParse")], sanitizer_type: Numeric }`. Wildcard receiver leverages the call-shape path in member_patterns_match (taint.rs:3077-3079). Mirrors the regex `\.(parse|safeParse)\s*\(` exactly (parity-preserving including the over-broad JSON.parse/Date.parse surface).

FIXED Gap 1 (M2-FIND-01 — cpp_std_stoi string-literal regression): replaced two CPP_AST_SANITIZERS blocks. Moved `("", "std::stoi")...("", "std::stod")` raw-substring entries to `call_names: ["std::stoi","std::stol","std::stoul","std::stoll","std::stof","std::stod"]`. Same for static_cast<T>: moved to `call_names: ["static_cast<int>", ..., "static_cast<double>"]`. Restricts matching to call_node_kinds descendants (call_expression for C++); string literals are not call_expressions, so the raw-fallback-in-string-literal regression cannot recur. Verified extract_call_name_c on tree-sitter-cpp returns the function-field text verbatim (qualified_identifier -> `std::stoi`; template_function -> `static_cast<int>`).

ADDED 4 new tests: typescript_zod_parse_sanitizer_truncates_flow_via_compute_taint + typescript_zod_safeParse_sanitizer_truncates_flow_via_compute_taint in tests/sanitize_breaks_flow_per_language.rs; mirrors typescript_zod_parse_sanitizer_truncates_flow_ast_only + typescript_zod_safeParse_sanitizer_truncates_flow_ast_only in tests/sanitize_breaks_flow_ast_only_harness.rs. All 4 PASS post-M3.

ast_only_harness.after_m3: 20 passed / 0 failed (18 baseline preserved + 2 new Zod tests)

regular_per_language.before_m3_at_m2_head: 20 passed / 15 failed (per M2-report.json — 19 truncates + 1 cpp_std_stoi PASS, 14 string-literal REDs + 1 cpp regression FAIL)
regular_per_language.delta: +3 PASS: (1) cpp_std_stoi_in_string_literal_does_not_sanitize PASS (Gap 1 fixed); (2) typescript_zod_parse_sanitizer_truncates_flow_via_compute_taint PASS (new); (3) typescript_zod_safeParse_sanitizer_truncates_flow_via_compute_taint PASS (new). 14 string-literal RED gates remain FAIL pre-M4 (expected, regex still active for non-cpp langs). 19 sanitize_breaks truncates_flow tests still PASS.

preexisting_unrelated_failures_unchanged: rr_module_function_integ_test::ruby_io_popen_with_user_input_via_compute_taint (pre-existing AST source-side gap, unrelated to sanitizer dispatch — same as M2)

ast_tests / fs_tests / git_tests / language_parity_test / metrics_tests / quality_tests / val010_change_impact_git_path_test / bench_surface_search_multilang / kotlin/swift language_status doctests (pre-existing failures from other workstreams, no taint connection — same as M2)

loc_delta_actual.vs_contract_estimate: Within budget — 2 gap fixes + 4 new tests + dense audit-comments documenting why the call_names migration is string-literal-safe and why the wildcard ('*','parse') is parity-preserving.

files_changed: crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs (+45 -22 net; 2 AstSanitizerPattern blocks modified for Cpp; 1 added for TypeScript)

discoveries.title: Latent raw-fallback string-literal collision risk persists for 13 langs (Rust .parse::<>, Ruby CGI.escapeHTML, Elixir String.to_integer, ...)
discoveries.summary: The M2-FIND-01 defect class (raw-substring fallback at member_patterns_match L3094-3098 matching descendant assignment text including string-literal children) was fixed for Cpp by moving entries to call_names. The same defect class is latent for any AstSanitizerPattern with non-empty pat_field + empty pat_rcv. M1 RED tests for those 13 langs were already FAILING at HEAD pre-M4 (regex still active matches inside string literals); they will need to GREEN at M4 — depending on whether the AST raw-fallback collides with their string-literal fixtures, M4 may need to apply the same Option-C migration (or a caller-side range-overlap filter) to the affected langs. Out of scope for M3 — documented here so M4 can address holistically.

compliance.staging_method: explicit add only (taint.rs + 2 test files + 4 reports)
compliance.no_regex_additions: OK (M3 is regex-DELETION milestone — only AST entries added)
compliance.no_build_sanitizer_ast_index_modificatio: OK (M2 added, only M4 may flip)
